title: Quantitative Methods in Archaeology
author: Bjørn Peare Bartholdy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Slides used in the course Quantitative Methods in Archaeology. 

 The slides accompanied the practical portion of course, which focused on teaching introductory statistics in R to MSc students at the Faculty of Archaeology, Leiden University. 

  The data files required to follow along can be found here:   https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7534850  

 The GitHub repo and HTML versions of the slides can be found here:  https://github.com/bbartholdy/quantarch-2022-slides  

 The files contain the following presentations: 

  lab-lecture_1.pdf  - Introduction to R: Part 1 

  lab-lecture_2.pdf  - Introduction to R: Part 2 

  lab-lecture_3.pdf  - Data Wrangling in R 

  lab-lecture_4.pdf  - Data Visualisation with ggplot2 

  lab-lecture_5.pdf  - Hypothesis Testing 

  lab-lecture_6.pdf  - Skeletons of Guildhall Yard (statistical case-study)
